mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals sustained consumer demand strength that translates into higher corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 45 percent of triggered instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment and an elevated federal funds rate signal a late-cycle contraction where restrictive policy coincides with labor market deterioration, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month that neither variable predicts alone.
failure_condition: The two-factor interaction of UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to add incremental explanatory power (delta R-squared < 0.01) over single-factor models in any 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals genuine supply-side repair rather than noise, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during post-shock months is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP and rising consumer prices indicate stagflationary pressure that squeezes corporate margins and constrains monetary policy, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling GDPC1 and rising CPIAUCSL produces positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 40 percent of observations over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate larger than 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers broad risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2pp are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index raises expected discount rates and terminal policy rates, reducing the present value of future equity cash flows and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day SPX returns is positive and statistically significant at the 5 percent level over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports sustained corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to monotonically predict 60-day SPX return direction with Kendall rank correlation below 0.05 over a 10-year sample.
